scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued. The suit property is situated within Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court. The suit is governed by Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as it relates to immovable property situated within the local limits of this Court's jurisdiction. The suit is filed in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it.

LIMITATION

2. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE AGREEMENT

3. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft.
4. The total consideration agreed upon was Rs. 85,00,000/-. The Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- on the date of the agreement via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654).
5. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against execution of the sale deed.
6. The Agreement to Sell is documented in the original agreement dated 10.01.2024, marked as Annexure A. The payment of advance is evidenced by the RTGS receipt, marked as Annexure B.

PLAINTIFF'S READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

7. The Plaintiff has always been and continues to be ready and willing to perform her part of the contract.
8. The Plaintiff has arranged the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- and is prepared to pay the same upon execution of the sale deed.
9. The Plaintiff has taken all necessary steps to perform her obligations, including keeping the funds available and preparing the requisite documents for registration.

DEFENDANT'S REFUSAL TO PERFORM

10. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming he received a higher offer.
11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed.
12. The Defendant refused to comply with the legal notice. The Defendant's refusal letter is marked as Annexure D, and the legal notice with postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to specific performance under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as the contract is in writing and duly executed.
14. The Plaintiff has continuously performed and been ready to perform her part of the contract as required under Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.
15. The contract is valid under Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
16. The Plaintiff seeks compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in the alternative, if specific performance is not granted.
17. The Plaintiff also prays for possession under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.
18. The agreement is admissible for part performance under Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.
19. Time is not of the essence in this contract for immovable property, and the Plaintiff is entitled to enforce the contract.

CAUSE OF ACTION

20. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant refused to execute the sale deed.
21. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to perform his obligations despite the legal notice dated 20.06.2024.
22. The suit is filed within the limitation period prescribed under Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

23. The suit is valued at {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee.
24. The court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid under the {{COURT_FEE_ACT_NAME}}.
25. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

26. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Direct the Defendant to specifically perform the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 and execute the sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff for the suit property;
(b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff;
(c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 CPC;
(e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF SUIT PROPERTY

{{SCHEDULE_OF_SUIT_PROPERTY -- Address, Survey/Plot No., Extent/Area, Boundaries (East/West/North/South)}}

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 26 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

C.S. No. {{CASE_NUMBER}} / 20{{YEAR}}

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- which is above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

2. The Plaintiff states that it has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The mediation certificate details are provided in Annexure P-5. [OR: The Plaintiff seeks exemption from pre-institution mediation as urgent interim relief is being sought.]

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The cause of action arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a written Software Development Agreement for development of an ERP system for a contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-, copy whereof is filed as Annexure P-1.
5. The Plaintiff duly performed all obligations required on its part under the agreement, specifically paying the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- via NEFT (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) on 01.02.2024.
6. Under the terms of the agreement, the Defendant was required to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024.
7. The Defendant failed to perform the contractual obligations on 15.06.2024 and abandoned the project without returning the advance.
8. The Plaintiff issued legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to perform its obligations, but the Defendant did not comply. A copy of the notice is filed as Annexure P-2.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

9. By reason of the Defendant's failure to perform, the Plaintiff has suffered actual and direct financial loss.
10. The Plaintiff quantifies the said loss at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rs. 20,00,000 refund + Rs. 12,00,000 consequential damages), and the computation with supporting material is set out in Annexure P-3.

COURT FEE AND VALUATION

11. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on the plaint.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Refund of Advance Payment
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: NEFT Receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)
     Documentary proof: Annexure P-4

(ii) Consequential Damages
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Loss assessment report by auditor
     Documentary proof: Annexure P-3

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

PRAYER

The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for:
(a) Recovery of Rs. 32,00,000/- towards damages and refund;
(b) Interest at 18% per annum from 15.06.2024 till realization;
(c) Costs of the suit;
(d) Any other relief this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure P-1 — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024
Annexure P-2 — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024
Annexure P-3 — Loss assessment report by auditor
Annexure P-4 — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)
Annexure P-5 — Email correspondence showing abandonment
Annexure P-6 — Mediation Certificate / Exemption Application

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}}.

I, Priya Menon, the Director and authorised representative of the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 11 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Priya Menon
DIRECTOR
PLAINTIFF

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, Priya Menon, the Plaintiff / authorised representative of the Plaintiff herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 11 are true to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

I understand that I shall be liable to proceedings under law for making a false statement.

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}}.

                                    Priya Menon
                                    PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}
